joke: I told my suitcase there’d be no vacation this year. Now I’m dealing with emotional baggage.
I’m reading a book about anti-gravity. It’s impossible to put down.
I only know 25 letters of the alphabet. I don’t know y.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field.
I used to hate facial hair, but then it grew on me.
I entered a hide-and-seek tournament, but the competition was hard to find.
I used to be addicted to soap, but I’m clean now.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
What did one wall say to the other? “I’ll meet you at the corner.”
Why did the computer get cold? It left its Windows open.
Why did the tomato blush? It saw the salad dressing.
I told a chemistry joke, but there was no reaction.
Why did the coffee call the police? It got mugged.
I tried to catch some fog yesterday. I mist.
Why don’t oysters donate to charity? Because they’re shellfish.
I bought a ceiling fan. Total waste of money—he just stands there applauding.
I used to play piano by ear, but now I use my hands.
My calendar’s days are numbered.
Parallel lines have so much in common. It’s a shame they’ll never meet.
I told my doctor I broke my arm in two places. He told me to stop going to those places.
I got fired from the orange juice factory. I couldn’t concentrate.
Why did the belt get arrested? It held up a pair of pants.
The shovel was a groundbreaking invention.
Why did the banker quit his job? He lost interest.
I stayed up all night wondering where the sun went. Then it dawned on me.
Why did the picture go to jail? It was framed.
I tried to organize a professional hide-and-seek league, but good players are hard to find.
What do you call a fake noodle? An impasta.
Why are ghosts such bad liars? You can see right through them.
What do you call a factory that makes decent products? Satisfactory.
